source.title: Jedno ti nedostaje: EP 60 - Dolazi oluja - fra Ante Vučković i Stanko Stojić

summary.title_hr: Jedno ti nedostaje: EP 60 - Dolazi oluja - fra Ante Vučković i Stanko Stojić
summary.abstract_hr: Epizoda analizira eshatološku besjedu Isusa iz Markova evanđelja (Mk 13, 1-23), fokusirajući se na proročanstvo o razorenju jeruzalemskog Hrama i nadolazećim nevoljama. Razgovor obuhvaća upozorenja o lažnim prorocima, ratovima, gladi i progonima, te pruža duhovne smjernice vjernicima kako se nositi s takvim izazovima. Naglašava se važnost ustrajnosti u vjeri, oslanjanja na Duha Svetoga i razumijevanja Krista kao novog Hrama, čak i u najtežim vremenima.
key_points: Isus je prorekao da od jeruzalemskog Hrama neće ostati 'ni kamen na kamenu nerazvaljen', što je za učenike bilo šokantno jer su Hram smatrali središtem svijeta i Božjeg boravišta.
Isus upozorava na zavodnike i lažne proroke koji će dolaziti u njegovo ime, naglašavajući potrebu za oprezom i razlikovanjem u Crkvi.
Nevolje poput ratova, gladi i potresa Isus naziva 'početkom trudova', a progoni vjernika bit će prilika za naviještanje Evanđelja svim narodima.
Učenici su pozvani da 'paze sami na sebe', što znači čuvati unutarnji mir i oslanjati se na Duha Svetoga koji će im dati pravu riječ u teškim trenucima progona.
Razorenje Jeruzalema 70. godine, koje je detaljno opisao Josip Flavije, kršćani su razumjeli kao ispunjenje Isusovog proročanstva i događaj koji je doveo do razdvajanja židovstva i kršćanstva.
Glad tijekom opsade Jeruzalema dovela je do strašnih zvjerstava i gubitka razuma, što je primjer kako ekstremne nevolje mogu promijeniti ljudsko ponašanje.
Za kršćane, nestanak Hrama ne predstavlja veliku novost jer je Krist uskrsli Hram, a Božja prisutnost je posvuda gdje se ljudi vole, prihvaćaju i slave liturgiju.
summary.title_en: One thing you lack: EP 60 - A storm is coming - fra Ante Vučković and Stanko Stojić
summary.abstract_en: The episode analyzes the eschatological discourse of Jesus from Mark's Gospel (Mk 13, 1-23), focusing on the prophecy about the destruction of the Jerusalem Temple and impending troubles. The discussion covers warnings about false prophets, wars, famine, and persecutions, and provides spiritual guidance to believers on how to deal with such challenges. The importance is emphasized of perseverance in faith, reliance on the Holy Spirit, and understanding Christ as the new Temple, even in the most difficult times.
key_points_en: Jesus prophesied that 'not one stone upon another untorn down' would remain of the Jerusalem Temple, which was shocking for the disciples because they considered the Temple the center of the world and God's dwelling place.
Jesus warns of deceivers and false prophets who will come in his name, emphasizing the need for caution and discernment in the Church.
Troubles like wars, famine, and earthquakes Jesus calls 'the beginning of birth pains', and persecutions of believers will be an opportunity for proclaiming the Gospel to all nations.
The disciples are called to 'watch out for themselves,' which means to preserve inner peace and to rely on the Holy Spirit who will give them the right word in difficult moments of persecution.

The destruction of Jerusalem in the year 70, which Josephus Flavius described in detail, Christians understood as the fulfillment of Jesus' prophecy and an event that led to the separation of Judaism and Christianity.
Hunger during the siege of Jerusalem led to terrible atrocities and loss of reason, which is an example of how extreme hardships can change human behavior.

For Christians, the disappearance of the Temple does not represent a great novelty because Christ is the risen Temple, and God's presence is everywhere where people love each other, accept each other, and celebrate the liturgy.
